summary: Systems architecture review cannot accept M4 withdrawal/postwar handoff yet. The diff stays inside allowed implementation/process paths and the required gates pass locally, but the implementation currently allows objective-complete postwar candidate creation without proving an M4 completed result, can lose carried grain on orderly withdrawal by treating DistrictId as PopulationGroupId, and reintroduces read-model aliasing through shallow WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate query DTO copies.

tests_run.result: On feat/m4-withdrawal-postwar-handoff with uncommitted task changes in allowed paths plus untracked writer handoff and new withdrawal test.

Tracked diff is limited to packages/protocol, packages/sim-core, tests/protocol-command-query-schema.test.ts, and allowed project task/message/routing state files. No forbidden tracked path was modified.
Vitest passed: 1 file, 8 tests, including withdrawal command/query schema coverage.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 32 files and 218 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file and 3 tests passed.
Task JSON validated successfully in REVIEW state.

decisions: Return REQUEST_CHANGES rather than ACCEPT because the current WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate path is not strictly derived from a validated completed M4 campaign result.

No hard-exclusion implementation was found: no full diplomacy, peace treaty system, rebellion system, formal historical content, campaign planning UI, M5 content, manual node battle, complex naval warfare, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, save-format package change, or parallel postwar settlement model was added.
The protocol additions are explicit serializable command/query DTOs and the sim-core/React/Pixi/Electron/Node IO boundary remains intact.

risks.issue: sim.resolve-m4-campaign-withdrawal rejects non-objective withdrawals from completed campaigns, but it does not reject objective-complete withdrawals from planned or active campaigns. createWarOutcomeIfNeeded then creates a WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate from only a district target and distinct local polity, so a caller can manufacture a postwar candidate without a completed siege/engagement/campaign result.

orderly withdrawal returns march.supply.carriedGrain to M2 with returnByPopulationGroupId.set(march.originDistrictId, ...). DistrictId and PopulationGroupId are branded as different IDs, and existing grain release code returns by reservation.source.populationGroupId. A valid state whose source population group ID differs from the origin district ID will zero march carriedGrain with supplyLoss 0 and fail to credit any M2 group, breaking conservation/loss accounting.

copyM4WarOutcomeReadModel shallow-copies outcome and postwarCandidate. outcome.reasonCodes, postwarCandidate.validM3Methods, and postwarCandidate.reasonCodes remain shared arrays with authoritative state, reintroducing the Query read-model aliasing class previously rejected in SIM-003.

The new scenario tests cover orderly withdrawal, rainy-season cancellation, supply-forced retreat, siege-lift withdrawal, and WarOutcome handoff, but do not cover the required failed-extraction withdrawal kind.

blockers: Add validation so objective-complete postwar handoff is accepted only when supported by an actual completed M4 campaign result, such as a completed campaign with valid surrendered siege/engagement references, and add rejection coverage for premature objective-complete commands.

Fix orderly-withdrawal grain accounting to use explicit grain source references or another auditable source ledger instead of assuming DistrictId equals PopulationGroupId; add a regression with nonmatching district and population group IDs proving conservation and loss accounting.

Deep-copy all nested arrays/objects exposed by sim.list-m4-war-outcomes, including outcome.reasonCodes and candidate arrays, and add a regression proving mutation of returned query DTOs cannot mutate runtime.world or invalidate validateWorldStateV0.
Add focused coverage for failed-extraction, including reason codes and troop conservation.
requested_action: Fix the listed withdrawal/postwar handoff correctness and read-model boundary issues, rerun the required M4-WITHDRAWAL-POSTWAR-HANDOFF-001 gates, and return a fresh REVIEW handoff to systems_architect.
